chunks.text: Avishay Tal is a member of Berkeley's Theory Group. He received his Ph.D. in 2015 from the Weizmann Institute of Science under the guidance of Ran Raz. Prior to that, he completed a Master's degree at the Technion under the guidance of Amir Shpilka. He has held postdoctoral appointments at the Institute for Advanced Study (hosted by Avi Wigderson), Simons Institute (as part of the Lower Bounds in Computational Complexity program), and Stanford University (hosted by Omer Reingold).
chunks.question: Where did Avishay Tal earn his PhD and who was his advisor?

chunks.text: His research interests are in computational complexity, analysis of boolean functions, circuit complexity, formula complexity, query complexity, pseudorandomness, learning, combinatorics, quantum computing, and the connections between algorithms and lower bounds.
chunks.question: What are Avishay Tal's main research interests?
chunks.answer: Computational complexity and related theory areas

chunks.text: Education: 2015, PhD, Computer Science, Weizmann Institute of Science. 2012, MS, Computer Science, The Technion, Haifa, Israel. 2007, BA, Mathematics, The Technion, Haifa, Israel. 2005, BS, Software Engineering, The Technion, Haifa, Israel.
chunks.question: What degrees did Avishay Tal earn from the Technion?

chunks.text: Research Areas: Theory (THY). Research Centers: Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC). Selected Honors and Awards: Sloan Research Fellow, 2022.
chunks.question: What honor did Avishay Tal receive in 2022?
